title: Ridgeline
lead: A longtime local bar with a loyal crowd and a brand that hadn't been touched since it opened. We built them an identity worth being proud of — and the photos to prove it.

sections.heading: Good bar. Forgettable brand.
paragraphs: Ridgeline Tavern had been a fixture in Hawley for years. Real food, good prices, the kind of place locals actually go. But their brand told a different story — a pixelated logo on a clipart sign, inconsistent fonts across their menus and Facebook page, and photos that made the food look worse than it tasted.

The owner knew the place had something. **She just couldn't show anyone what it was.** New visitors would Google them, see the outdated presence, and either move on or show up with low expectations — neither of which she wanted.

callout.label: The Problem
callout.body: No consistent visual identity across any touchpoint. Photography that undersold a genuinely good experience. Nothing that communicated what made Ridgeline worth the drive.

sections.heading: Start with what's already true.
paragraphs: The best brand work doesn't invent a personality — it finds the one that already exists and builds a visual system around it. Ridgeline had a clear identity: **unpretentious, local, no-nonsense**. Good food, cold drinks, no dress code. The brand just needed to say that clearly.

We started with a conversation about who their customers actually are, what keeps them coming back, and what kind of new customer they were trying to attract. From there, everything — colors, typography, photography style — followed from that core.

callout.label: The Direction
callout.body: Warm, grounded, and confident. Nothing trendy. A brand that feels like the place itself — lived-in and worth returning to.

sections.heading: A visual identity built to last.
paragraphs: We developed a full brand package — logo system, color palette, typography, and usage guidelines — designed to work consistently across every surface they use, from menus to social media to signage.
afterItems: Clean logo with primary + alternate marks

sections.heading: Images that match the brand.
paragraphs: New branding only goes so far if the photos still look like a flip phone took them. We shot a full session at Ridgeline — food, atmosphere, behind the bar — with the brand direction in mind from the start. Warm light, real moments, nothing staged to the point of feeling fake.
**The goal was simple:** someone should be able to look at these photos and immediately understand what kind of place Ridgeline is, and want to be there.

pullQuote.text: "The photos finally looked like the place we'd been running for ten years."

sections.heading: Looks like what it always was.
paragraphs: Ridgeline walked away with a complete brand identity and a full photography library — everything they needed to show up consistently online, in print, and in person. The brand didn't change who they were. It just finally said it clearly.
Within a week of updating their Facebook and Google listing with the new photos and logo, the owner reported the most engagement she'd seen on a post in years — without running a single ad.

callout.label: What They Left With
callout.body: A brand identity they own outright, photography assets ready for any use, and a visual presence that finally matches the quality of their actual business.

cta.heading: Ready to look like
cta.sub: Every project starts with a conversation. Reach out and I'll get back to you within 24 hours.
